design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from four X-Men characters, navigate platforming challenges, defeat boss enemies, manage limited mutant power bar for special abilities, switch characters strategically mid-game
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mutant power bar depletion forcing reliance on basic attacks; character switching mechanics creating tactical resource management within platforming action
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic X-Men team members (Wolverine, Cyclops, Gambit, Nightcrawler) utilizing their signature powers and weapons in coordinated combat scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid design blending platforming, tactics, fighting, and strategy into single experience; character abilities deeply tied to Marvel lore (Wolverine's healing factor, Nightcrawler's teleportation as level progression tool); support character system adds team-based tactical layer unusual for genre

difficulty.lives_system: Standard life bar per character; characters can be switched to restore fresh power bars; healing factor available via Wolverine's passive ability
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides difficulty modulation; Nightcrawler's teleportation enables sequence breaking and area skipping; player choice of which character to deploy affects tactical approach to obstacles

progression.upgrade_system: Mutant power bar regenerates slowly when depleted; character switching resets individual character power bars to last-used state
progression.unlock_mechanism: Support characters (Storm, Rogue, Iceman, Archangel) available for tactical calls; Jean Grey supports with pickup function for fallen characters
replayability_hooks: Four distinct playable characters with unique movesets encourage alternate playthroughs
Character switching mechanics reward experimentation with different tactical approaches
Nightcrawler's teleportation enables sequence breaking for skilled players

world_building_techniques: Game manual provides framing narrative: Danger Room training simulator infected with computer virus
Villain encounters with familiar X-Men adversaries provide contextual storytelling
Magneto revelation as final boss tie-in to broader X-Men mythology

boss_design.philosophy: Familiar X-Men villains serve as level-ending encounters; difficulty noted as 'near-impossible-to-beat' by critical reception; boss patterns require memorization and precise execution
notable_bosses: Mojo (features infamous 'Crunch' segment requiring Genesis reset button exploit)

faction_indicators: Four selectable X-Men characters represent different team archetypes: claw fighter (Wolverine), energy projectile user (Cyclops), staff combatant (Gambit), teleport specialist (Nightcrawler)
danger_signals: Unfairly placed enemies identified by critics
environmental_storytelling: Danger Room setting establishes training combat environment with evolving hazards from virus-infected systems
Sauron cameo appearance providing incidental ally interaction
